meta_title: Impact Doors in Kendall, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Protect your Kendall home with HVHZ-rated impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. ESW & CWI brands, expert installation, free estimates.
h1: Kendall's Trusted Source for Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors That Stand Up to South Florida's Worst
intro:
Kendall, Florida is one of Miami-Dade County's most desirable suburban communities — a sprawling, tree-lined landscape of well-maintained neighborhoods like The Hammocks, Kendale Lakes, Kendall East, and Sunset where average home values hover around $450,000. Families are drawn here for top-rated schools, proximity to Baptist Hospital, weekend afternoons at Kendall Indian Hammocks Park, and the convenience of Dadeland Mall just minutes away. But beneath the comfortable, established feel of this community lies a reality every Kendall homeowner must reckon with: this area sits squarely within one of the most hurricane-exposed corridors in the entire United States, and your doors are the first line of defense when a major storm makes its approach.

Unlike some parts of South Florida where impact-resistant products are a smart upgrade, in Kendall they are a critical necessity. The community sits roughly 10 miles from the coast, which means it receives little of the geographic protection that more inland areas might enjoy, yet also lacks the direct beachfront exposures that sometimes prompt earlier action. That in-between position has historically led some homeowners to delay upgrades — a decision that hurricanes like Andrew, Wilma, and Irma have proven time and again to be costly. With 45% of Kendall's housing stock built before 1994 — before modern hurricane-resistant building codes were enacted — a significant portion of homes in neighborhoods like Kendale Lakes and Kendall West are still protected by original doors that were never designed to handle HVHZ-rated wind loads.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in bringing those older homes — and newer ones alike — into full compliance with Florida's most stringent hurricane protection standards. Our impact doors aren't just a safety product; they are a long-term investment in your home's structural integrity, energy efficiency, resale value, and insurance savings. From impact-resistant entry doors to sliding glass doors and elegant French door systems, we install only the highest-rated products available for Miami-Dade's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, ensuring Kendall families can ride out whatever the Atlantic throws their way with confidence and peace of mind.

section_why.body:

With more than 60 years of combined installation experience among our crew and over 500 homes successfully completed throughout South Florida, we bring a depth of local knowledge that generic window-and-door companies simply cannot match.

In Kendall specifically, we understand the unique challenges that come with the territory. Many homes in Kendale Lakes, The Hammocks, and Kendall East feature older architectural styles — wide sliding glass door openings, ornate entry door frames, and custom French door configurations that require precision measurement, careful structural assessment, and expert fitting. Our team has worked extensively throughout Miami-Dade County and is thoroughly familiar with the Miami-Dade County Building Department's permitting requirements, HVHZ product approval standards, and inspection protocols. We handle the permit process from start to finish, so homeowners never have to navigate that paperwork maze on their own.

Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau isn't a marketing claim — it's the result of thousands of interactions with real homeowners who expected the best and received it. We pride ourselves on transparent, upfront communication, clean and efficient job sites, and post-installation support that extends well beyond the day we pack up our equipment. Whether you're in a 1980s ranch home in Kendall West or a newer two-story in The Palms at Town & Country area, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the same rigorous standards, the same quality brands, and the same commitment to craftsmanship that has made us the Kings of Service across South Florida.

We also make impact door installation financially accessible for Kendall families at every budget level. With financing options starting at just $87 per month and $0 down payment programs available, there's no reason to delay the protection your home and family deserve. Our team will walk you through every option, provide a detailed no-pressure estimate, and help you make the decision that makes the most sense for your specific situation.

section_history.h2: Kendall's Storm History Makes a Powerful Case for Impact Door Protection
section_history.body:
No community in South Florida understands the destructive potential of Atlantic hurricanes better than Kendall. In August 1992, Hurricane Andrew made landfall just south of the area and delivered a generational catastrophe to Miami-Dade County. With sustained winds exceeding 165 mph and gusts reaching 177 mph, Andrew obliterated tens of thousands of homes across the county and served as the defining moment that forced Florida lawmakers and building officials to completely overhaul the state's construction standards. Kendall neighborhoods absorbed significant damage during that storm, and the community spent years rebuilding. Homes that existed before 1994 and have not since been upgraded often still carry the vulnerabilities that Andrew so brutally exposed — and in Kendall, that represents nearly half of all residential properties.

The region has faced 11 named storms since 1990, with Hurricane Wilma in 2005 and Hurricane Irma in 2017 serving as the most recent painful reminders of how quickly a comfortable suburban neighborhood can be transformed into a disaster zone. Wilma's rapid intensification and wide damage swath caught many Kendall residents off guard, with wind-driven debris punching through unprotected entry doors and sliding glass panels, allowing catastrophic water intrusion that compounded structural damage. Irma, though slightly less intense at landfall, brought sustained tropical-storm-force and hurricane-force gusts that tested even some upgraded homes. In both storms, properties protected by HVHZ-rated impact doors showed dramatically lower rates of interior damage compared to those relying on traditional doors or even basic hurricane shutters.

The lesson from Kendall's storm history is clear and well-documented: passive, permanent protection — the kind that impact doors provide — outperforms reactive measures like accordion shutters and plywood boarding every time. Impact doors require no preparation, no storage, and no last-minute scramble when a storm is 48 hours out. They are always armed and always ready, which matters enormously when a storm like Irma compresses the preparation timeline and puts families in difficult, dangerous situations.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Kendall
section_code.body:
Kendall falls entirely within Miami-Dade County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, the most stringent hurricane construction designation in the United States. The HVHZ classification was established in the aftermath of Hurricane Andrew and imposes building standards that exceed the already-rigorous requirements of the Florida Building Code applied in other parts of the state. For homeowners in ZIP codes 33156, 33176, 33183, 33186, 33193, and 33196, this means that any impact door installed on a residential property must carry a valid Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) — a product-specific certification that confirms the door system has been independently tested and approved to withstand HVHZ wind loads and missile impact standards.

The Florida Building Code mandates that all exterior openings in HVHZ areas be protected against wind-borne debris traveling at speeds consistent with the area's design wind speed, which in Kendall reaches into the 170+ mph range for the most exposed structures. Impact doors must pass both large missile impact testing (simulating a 9-pound 2x4 traveling at 50 feet per second) and cyclic pressure testing that mimics the repeated stress of hurricane wind gusts. Products that meet these benchmarks receive their Miami-Dade NOA and are eligible for installation in the HVHZ. Generic products sold at big-box retailers often do not carry this certification and are not legally installable as hurricane protection in Kendall.

All impact door installations in Kendall require a permit pulled through the Miami-Dade County Building Department, followed by a mandatory inspection upon completion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting and inspection process on behalf of our clients, from submitting product approval documentation to scheduling the final sign-off. This full-service approach ensures your installation is not only safe and effective but also fully code-compliant — which matters enormously for insurance purposes, future home sales, and your legal standing in the event of a storm-related insurance claim.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: HVHZ-Rated Products Built for Kendall's Toughest Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted impact door brands in the South Florida market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers build their products specifically for the demands of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, and both carry the Miami-Dade NOA certifications required for legal installation throughout Kendall and the broader Miami-Dade County area. These are not mass-market products repurposed for hurricane country — they are purpose-engineered systems designed from the ground up to perform under the most extreme conditions the Atlantic can produce.

ESW's impact door lineup includes heavy-duty entry doors, sliding glass door systems, and French door configurations, all featuring laminated impact glass that bonds to an interlayer upon impact rather than shattering into dangerous projectiles. ESW door frames are constructed with reinforced aluminum or fiberglass profiles that resist bowing and racking under high wind pressure loads, and their multi-point locking systems engage at multiple points along the door frame rather than a single central latch — dramatically improving the door's resistance to being blown inward or outward under hurricane-force pressure differentials. For Kendall homeowners with large rear lanai openings or pool areas featuring expansive sliding glass doors, ESW's wide-format sliding systems offer a seamless blend of everyday functionality and storm-season security.

CWI's impact door products bring a complementary set of strengths to the table, particularly in the entry door category. Their fiberglass and steel door systems offer excellent thermal performance in addition to hurricane protection — a meaningful benefit in Kendall's subtropical climate, where air conditioning costs represent a significant portion of annual household expenses. CWI doors are engineered to minimize air infiltration and thermal transfer, reducing energy bills year-round while providing the structural backbone that HVHZ standards demand. Whether you're updating a single front door or replacing every exterior opening on a Kendall home, the combination of ESW and CWI gives Premier Impact Windows & Roofing the product depth to find the ideal solution for your specific architecture, budget, and aesthetic preferences.

section_cost.h2: What Kendall Homeowners Can Expect to Pay for Impact Doors — and Why They're Worth Every Dollar
section_cost.body:
Impact door pricing in Kendall varies based on door type, size, configuration, and the specific product selected, but homeowners can generally expect entry-level single impact entry doors to start in the $1,500–$2,500 range installed, while sliding glass door systems and French door configurations typically range from $3,000 to $8,000 or more depending on width and complexity. For a Kendall home averaging $450,000 in value, a full exterior door replacement — including the front entry, rear sliding glass door, and any additional access points — often represents an investment in the $8,000–$20,000 range. These figures can feel significant in the abstract, but context matters enormously when evaluating their true value.

Consider that impact doors eliminate the need to purchase, store, and install hurricane shutters — products that can cost thousands of dollars themselves and require significant physical labor to deploy before each storm. More importantly, impact doors begin generating measurable returns from day one through reduced homeowner's insurance premiums. Miami-Dade County homeowners with fully impact-protected homes regularly report annual insurance savings of $1,500 to $3,500 or more depending on their insurer, coverage levels, and wind mitigation report findings. Over a 10-year period, those savings can fully offset the cost of the door installation — meaning the protection essentially pays for itself while adding permanent value to your home.

For Kendall families who prefer not to pay the full cost upfront, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options with payments starting as low as $87 per month and $0 down payment programs available to qualified applicants. Our financing partners offer competitive terms that allow homeowners to begin enjoying the safety, energy efficiency, and insurance benefits of their new impact doors immediately, without waiting until the full cost has been saved. Protecting your Kendall home shouldn't be a question of affordability — and with our financing solutions, it doesn't have to be.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors in Kendall Translate to Real, Measurable Insurance Savings
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Miami-Dade County is among the most expensive in the nation, and Kendall residents feel that reality acutely every time their annual renewal arrives. Florida's property insurance market has been under sustained pressure for years, with several major carriers reducing coverage, raising rates, or exiting the state entirely. In that environment, every available discount becomes critically important — and impact doors represent one of the most significant and well-documented discount triggers available to South Florida homeowners.

Florida law requires insurers to offer premium credits to homeowners who install hurricane-protective opening protection that meets the standards of the Florida Building Code. Impact doors that carry a Miami-Dade NOA and are installed in accordance with HVHZ requirements qualify for these wind mitigation credits, which are formally documented through a Wind Mitigation Inspection Report submitted to your insurance carrier. The credits are applied to the wind portion of your premium — which in Kendall can represent 50–70% of your total homeowner's insurance cost — making the percentage savings quite substantial. Many Kendall homeowners report total premium reductions of 20–40% after completing a full impact door and window installation.

Major carriers serving the Kendall market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Heritage, Universal Property & Casualty, and others — all honor Florida's wind mitigation credit requirements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's installations are fully documented with product approval numbers, installation records, and permit sign-offs, giving you a clean, defensible paper trail when submitting your wind mitigation report. We can also advise you on coordinating the timing of your inspection to maximize the credit applied to your next renewal cycle. When you add insurance savings to the energy efficiency gains and the elimination of shutter costs, impact doors in Kendall deliver a financial return profile that very few home improvement investments can match.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact doors in Kendall, even though I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Yes, absolutely. Kendall falls entirely within Miami-Dade County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone regardless of its distance from the coastline. HVHZ designation is based on regional wind speed modeling and storm history, not coastal proximity alone. This means all exterior doors must meet Miami-Dade NOA certification standards to legally qualify as hurricane protection. Standard doors — even those marketed as 'hurricane-resistant' — may not meet HVHZ requirements and could leave your home exposed and your insurance claims at risk.

faqs.q: What does it cost to install impact doors in a Kendall home, and do you offer financing?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on door type and quantity, but most Kendall homeowners invest between $1,500 and $8,000+ per door opening installed, depending on whether it's a single entry door, sliding glass system, or French door configuration. A full home replacement typically ranges from $8,000 to $20,000. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available, making it easy to protect your home without a large upfront payment.

faqs.q: How does the permit process work for impact doors in Miami-Dade County?
faqs.a: All impact door installations in Kendall require a permit through the Miami-Dade County Building Department, along with documentation of the product's Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA). After installation, a mandatory inspection is required to confirm code compliance. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit and inspection process on your behalf — from submitting the application and product approvals to scheduling and passing the final inspection. You don't have to deal with any of the paperwork.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in my Kendall home?
faqs.a: The timeline from signed contract to completed installation typically ranges from 4 to 8 weeks in Kendall, depending on product lead times, permit processing speed at the Miami-Dade County Building Department, and the scope of the project. The physical installation itself is usually completed in one to two days for a standard residential door replacement. Our team coordinates every step — permitting, product delivery, installation, and inspection — and keeps you informed throughout the entire process.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact doors in Kendall?
faqs.a: Insurance savings vary by carrier, current coverage levels, and whether your entire home is impact-protected, but many Kendall homeowners with complete impact door installations report annual premium reductions of 20–40%. Given the high cost of Miami-Dade County homeowner's insurance — where wind coverage can represent more than half of your total premium — those savings are substantial. A certified Wind Mitigation Inspection following your installation documents the upgrades for your insurer. Over time, these savings frequently offset the full cost of the door installation.
